skill.name: full-review
skill.description: Run a comprehensive pre-commit code review by mapping changed files to relevant review checklists and producing a structured issue report.
skill.summary: Coordinates broad code review checks for changed files before commit or merge.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command and blocker patterns, but most are Markdown examples, inline file globs, checklist text, or report-format placeholders. The only confirmed behavior is read-only local git inspection with stderr redirected to /dev/null, which is expected for a code review skill and shows no network, credential access, or prompt-injection attempt.

low_findings.title: Read-Only Local Git Commands
low_findings.description: The workflow includes a fenced bash example that runs git diff and git status to identify changed files. This is legitimate for a review skill and does not show command injection, network access, or secret collection, but it can expose repository file names and status to the assistant context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in a Markdown bash block and are limited to local git inspection. They are read-only and contain no user-controlled shell concatenation.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Content
low_findings.description: The high blocker and many external command detections are caused by ordinary prose, inline backticks, file globs, skill names, checklist items, and report placeholders. No weak cryptographic implementation, system reconnaissance behavior, or executable Ruby backtick code is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown descriptions, tables, checklists, and output templates rather than executable code. No malicious semantics were found after reviewing the file context.

low_findings.title: Benign Standard Device Redirection
low_findings.description: The filesystem finding is stderr redirection to /dev/null in a git command example. This suppresses command errors and does not read files, write project data, or access sensitive paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only device-file reference is the standard shell redirection 2>/dev/null. It is attached to a read-only git diff command and has no data exfiltration path.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Shell Command Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the assistant to use local git commands for change discovery. The commands are expected for the workflow, but marketplace users should understand that using the skill may inspect repository metadata.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit and easy to verify. Risk is limited because the commands are read-only and do not include networking or dynamic input interpolation.

content.value_statement: Large changes can hide security, type safety, and performance issues before review. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured pre-commit review workflow.
actual_capabilities: Identifies changed files using local git diff and git status commands.
Maps file paths to relevant review skills and domain checklists.
Applies security, DeFi, type safety, performance, and code quality checks.
Produces a structured report with critical issues, warnings, suggestions, and readiness status.
Offers optional fixes for critical issues after showing the proposed change.
limitations: It depends on the review skills available in the active assistant environment.
It does not run tests or linters unless the assistant chooses additional commands.
It is tailored toward TypeScript, React, Rust, PostgreSQL, and DeFi projects.
It can miss issues that require runtime context, production data, or external system access.

use_cases.title: Pre-Commit Review
use_cases.description: Check local changes before committing and receive a clear list of blocking issues.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Quality Gate
use_cases.description: Review a branch for security, type safety, performance, and integration concerns before merging.

use_cases.title: Domain-Specific DeFi Audit
use_cases.description: Apply DeFi, blockchain, and RPC checks to changes that touch tokens, swaps, protocols, or pricing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Change Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a normal local review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use full-review to review my current changes before I commit. Focus on critical issues first.

prompt_templates.title: Focused Security Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review security and data handling risks in changed files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run full-review on my changed files. Prioritize SQL injection, XSS, command injection, secrets, validation, and rate limiting.

prompt_templates.title: Frontend and API Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Check a full-stack feature before merge.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use full-review for this branch. Check React states, TanStack Query usage, API schemas, database changes, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced DeFi Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Audit blockchain-related changes with domain checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run full-review on the changed protocol and trading files. Include token decimals, slippage, BigInt handling, multicall failures, and price validation.

output_examples.input: Review my current branch before I open a pull request.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists reviewed files, applied review areas, critical issues, warnings, suggestions, and a ready-to-merge decision.

output_examples.input: Check my DeFi swap changes for risky logic.
output: Slippage and revert handling are reviewed.
Critical fixes are separated from optional improvements.

best_practices: Run the skill after staging or saving all intended changes so the diff is complete.
Ask for a focused review area when the branch is large or time is limited.
Review proposed fixes before allowing the assistant to edit files.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the report as a replacement for tests, linters, or human code review.
Do not use it on unrelated large branches without narrowing the review scope.
Do not approve automatic fixes without reading the proposed change.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: It suggests local git commands to identify changed files. It does not define build, test, network, or deployment commands.

faq.question: Can it fix issues automatically?
faq.answer: It can offer fixes for critical issues. The workflow says to show the issue and proposed fix before applying changes.

faq.question: Which projects fit this skill best?
faq.answer: It fits TypeScript, React, Rust, PostgreSQL, and DeFi projects best because the checklist names those areas.

faq.question: Does it replace a security audit?
faq.answer: No. It is a structured code review helper and should be combined with testing, tooling, and expert review.

faq.question: Can Claude Code and Codex use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What output should I expect?
faq.answer: Expect a review report with files reviewed, skills applied, critical issues, warnings, suggestions, and commit readiness.
